description:
As a Maritime Training Librarian, catalog the Bridge Command environments and create a comprehensive training suite. Catalog at least three worlds found in /opt/bridgecommand/World/ into a reference document at /home/ga/Documents/world_catalog.txt including the title and author 'Maritime Training Librarian', the list of worlds, and suitability ratings (Beginner/Intermediate/Advanced) for each based on your assessment.

Create three distinct scenarios in /opt/bridgecommand/Scenarios/, each containing environment.ini, ownship.ini, and othership.ini files:
- Open Water Navigation: Located at /opt/bridgecommand/Scenarios/x1) Open Water Exercise/ using a suitable open water world. Set environment to daytime (10:00-14:00), moderate seas (Weather 3.0-5.0), and good visibility (>10nm). Own ship is 'MV Pacific Trader' at 12 kts with exactly 2 traffic vessels.
- Coastal Pilotage: Located at /opt/bridgecommand/Scenarios/x2) Coastal Pilotage Exercise/ using a different coastal/island world. Set environment to dawn (06:00-08:00), calm seas (Weather <= 2.0), and moderate visibility (3.0-7.0nm). Own ship is 'MV Dorado Star' at 8 kts with exactly 3 traffic vessels.
- Restricted Visibility: Located at /opt/bridgecommand/Scenarios/x3) Restricted Visibility Exercise/ using a third different world. Set environment to night (22:00-03:00), calm seas (Weather <= 1.5), and foggy conditions (Visibility 0.5-1.5nm). Own ship is 'MV Northern Express' at 10 kts with exactly 3 traffic vessels (Head-on, Crossing, and Overtaking).

Map these scenarios to STCW competencies in /home/ga/Documents/curriculum_mapping.txt. Finally, update the configuration in /opt/bridgecommand/bc5.ini to support the restricted visibility scenario with these radar settings: arpa_on=1, full_radar=1, radar_range_resolution=256, and max_radar_range=72.
